data.title: Agios' PYRUKYND® approved by CHMP for anemia in adults with thalassemia, enhancing treatment options.
data.description: Today, Agios revealed that the Committee for Medicinal Products for Human Use (CHMP) has provided a favorable opinion for the new indication of PYRUKYND in adult patients with anemia linked to thalassemia.

data.title: Oklo and newcleo partner with Blykalla for US nuclear fuel development; newcleo to invest $2 billion.
data.description: In Santa Clara, California, Oklo Inc. (NYSE: OKLO), an advanced nuclear technology company, and newcleo, a European developer of advanced nuclear reactors, have revealed a partnership to build advanced fuel fabrication and manufacturing infrastructure in the US. The agreement, contingent on standard approvals, will see newcleo investing up to $2 billion through a newcleo-affiliated investment entity. Additionally, Blykalla, an advanced nuclear technology developer in Sweden, is contemplating collaboration.

data.title: Shiba Inu drops 7% to $0.000009687, down 26% monthly. Charts suggest a possible 30% decline ahead unless bulls rebound.
data.description: Shiba Inu (CRYPTO: SHIB) has seen a 7% drop on Friday, trading at $0.000009687, indicating a monthly decline of over 26%. Analysis suggests a potential further 30% decrease if bulls do not rally soon. Shiba Inu has breached the critical support zone of $0.0000098–$0.0000100, leading to increased downward pressure. The daily chart reflects a significant break below the long-standing floor, with technical indicators pointing towards a bearish scenario unless there is a swift recovery. For more information, refer to a detailed report on the situation.

data.title: San Francisco officials and allies are criticizing Marc Benioff as new details on Salesforce's ties to Trump admin emerge.
data.description: San Francisco officials are expressing concern over recent disclosures regarding Salesforce's collaboration with the Trump administration. The company's alleged efforts have sparked backlash from both local authorities and supporters.

data.title: Equinor ASA is buying back shares for employee share programs (OSE:EQNR, NYSE:EQNR).
data.description: The Equinor ASA (OSE:EQNR, NYSE:EQNR) recently conducted transactions under its buy-back program to secure shares for employee and management share-based incentive programs. The buy-back involved a significant number of shares, in accordance with the company's commitment to rewarding and retaining valuable staff members. This initiative aims to boost employee morale and motivation by offering them an opportunity to benefit from the company's growth and success. The buy-back program is a strategic move by Equinor ASA to strengthen its workforce and drive performance through incentivized compensation packages.

data.title: Bitcoin drops 5%, Ethereum, XRP, Dogecoin fall 6% with crypto liquidations surpassing $1 billion. Bitcoin hits low at $103,856. ETFs witness net outflows - BTC $536.4 million, ETH $56.9 million. Traders predict solid buy orders between $100,000 and $104,000.
data.description: Bitcoin dropped to a low of $103,856 on Friday, leading to a significant increase in liquidations totaling $1.19 billion within the last 24 hours. Concurrently, spot BTC ETFs experienced net outflows of $536.4 million on Thursday, while ETH ETFs saw net outflows of $56.9 million. Market analyst Ted Pillows pointed out strong buy orders for Bitcoin ranging from $100,000 to $104,000 on Binance. Pillows anticipates bullish investors to defend this price zone, suggesting a potential retest to fill the previous week's wick and establish a robust support level for future movements. Further details are available on Benzinga.com.
